input: Find candidates with 7+ years of mortgage loan processing experience who also have underwriting skills.
expected_answer_summary: The candidate in 3547447.pdf has 7 years of mortgage loan processing and banking experience combined with 3 years of underwriting and lending experience. They held roles including Consumer Underwriter II and Loan Processor, working with FHA, conventional, and VA loans. They also bring 3 years of risk management and 3 years of compliance and quality assurance.

input: Tell me about the candidate in 3547447.pdf — what is their background and what roles have they held?
expected_answer_summary: The candidate is a Mortgage Banking Foreclosure Specialist with over 10 years of loan operations experience. They progressed from Loan Processor (2003–2008) and Loan Document Specialist II (2008–2010) to Consumer Underwriter II (2011–2013) and their current Foreclosure Specialist role (2014–present). Core skills include FHA/VA underwriting, loss mitigation, risk management, quality assurance, and proficiency in industry tools such as LPS, MSP, and SharePoint.

input: Compare the candidate in 3547447.pdf with the candidate in 10001727-2.pdf in terms of financial and operational background.
expected_answer_summary: The 3547447.pdf candidate is a financial services specialist with deep expertise in mortgage underwriting, loan processing, and foreclosure — all within banking. The 10001727-2.pdf candidate has operations and revenue management experience in the hospitality sector, holding an MBA from Stratford University, and is skilled in PBX, OPERA, and night-audit processes. The mortgage specialist offers deeper compliance and financial risk expertise, while the hospitality candidate brings multi-unit supervisory and revenue management strengths.

input: How many candidates in the pool have both auditing and quality assurance or quality control experience?
expected_answer_summary: At least two candidates in the pool explicitly list auditing and quality control or quality assurance experience. The candidate in 3547447.pdf holds 3 years in compliance and quality assurance alongside mortgage auditing roles. The candidate in 10001727-2.pdf performed night audits and financial transaction auditing in a hotel operations context.

input: Find candidates with hospitality management experience and a graduate degree who have worked in revenue management.

They worked as Front Desk Manager handling financial transactions and revenue management, and also served as a Sous Chef and Front Desk Agent across multiple properties, bringing broad hospitality and operations experience.

input: Tell me about the Sous Chef candidate — what certifications do they hold and what is their educational background?

Their certifications include TIPS Certified, CPR-AED, Certified Food Handler, Certified Culinarian, and Food Management Professional (State of Virginia). They have hands-on experience in culinary arts, hotel front desk operations, night auditing, and IHG Rewards program management.

input: Compare the hospitality candidate (10001727-2.pdf) and the social work candidate (10062724-6.pdf) on supervisory and management experience.
expected_answer_summary: The 10001727-2.pdf candidate supervised front-of-house staff as a Shift Supervisor, conducted performance evaluations, and managed staffing for hotel properties. The 10062724-6.pdf candidate supervised a 24/7 psychiatric crisis call center, managing staff licensing, scheduling, performance indicators, and hiring/termination — a much larger scope. Both have strong supervisory credentials, but the social worker's managerial breadth in a regulated healthcare environment is significantly more extensive.

input: How many candidates in the pool have food, culinary, or restaurant-sector experience?
expected_answer_summary: Only one candidate in the pool has documented culinary or food-service experience. The candidate in 10001727-2.pdf worked as a Sous Chef and Prep Cook and holds certifications including Certified Culinarian and Certified Food Handler (State of Virginia). No other resumes in the pool indicate restaurant or culinary backgrounds.

input: Find candidates with 9+ years of experience in energy engineering, HVAC systems, and performance contracting.

In their current Sr. Estimator role in the USA, they generate approximately $2M in estimates per month on capital construction projects using Studio 360. The candidate in 10041713-5.pdf worked as a Project Engineer designing, developing, and testing DCS, PLC, and SCADA programs for Honeywell systems and also lists Variable Frequency Drives and industrial automation knowledge. input: Compare Marco Gonzalez (resume_04434-19.pdf) with the social worker candidate (10062724-6.pdf) for a healthcare leadership role requiring both clinical expertise and data analytics.

input: Find research scientists in Germany with a PhD in Physics, nanotechnology expertise, and publications in quantum physics.
expected_answer_summary: Timo Hentsch (Timo-21.pdf) is a Scientist specializing in nondestructive testing based in Germany, currently at Rosenxt Group since September 2023. He holds a Dr. rer. nat. in Physics from Leibniz Universität Hannover and a Diplom-Ingenieur in Nanotechnology from Julius-Maximilians-Universität Würzburg, and was a Harvard University Research Fellow (2011–2012). He has five peer-reviewed publications in quantum transport, quantum dots, and shot noise in quantum systems.

input: Give me a full profile of Timo Hentsch (Timo-21.pdf) — his research background, publications, and career timeline.
expected_answer_summary: Timo Hentsch (Timo-21.pdf) has a distinguished research career: after a Nanotechnology degree from Würzburg and a Physics doctorate from Hannover (2013–2018), he was a Wissenschaftlicher Mitarbeiter at Leibniz Universität Hannover for nearly 6 years and a Harvard Research Fellow for one year. He joined ROSEN in 2018 as Scientist in Lingen, Germany, and transitioned to Rosenxt Group in 2023. His five publications cover quantum transport, Hund's rule interactions, shot noise control, and superconductivity in quantum spin Hall systems. He is a native German speaker with Full Professional English.

input: Compare Timo Hentsch (Timo-21.pdf) and Sebastian Müller (Sebastian-20.pdf) for a senior R&D leadership role at a technology company in Germany.
expected_answer_summary: Timo Hentsch (Timo-21.pdf) is a research-first scientist with a Physics doctorate, Harvard fellowship, five quantum physics publications, and ongoing NDT research at Rosenxt Group. Sebastian Müller (Sebastian-20.pdf) holds a Ph.D. from Universität Hamburg, an M.S. in Photonics, and a Diplom-Ingenieur in Laser Technology, and is currently CTO of Rosenxt Group with 9+ years at ROSEN — including Head of Technology Platform. Müller is the stronger candidate for a strategic R&D leadership or CTO-type role; Hentsch is the better fit for a principal scientist or deep-research position.

input: How many candidates in the pool have academic research experience (university positions, fellowships, or publications)?

Sebastian Müller (Sebastian-20.pdf) completed a Ph.D. at Universität Hamburg and holds advanced degrees in Photonics and Laser Technology from Fachhochschule Münster, though no academic position post-doctorate is listed. The 10062724-6.pdf candidate also has documented academic teaching as Behavioral Science Faculty in a Family Practice Residency Program from 1992 to 1996.

input: Find CTO-level candidates in the Münster region of Germany with a PhD and background in photonics or optical engineering.

He holds a Ph.D. from Universität Hamburg, an M.S. in Photonics from Fachhochschule Münster, and a Diplom-Ingenieur in Laser Technology and Optics from Fachhochschule Münster. He served at ROSEN for over 9 years — including 3+ years as Head of Technology Platform and 6 years as Sensor Developer — before becoming CTO at Rosenxt Group in October 2023. His top skills include Business Development and Consulting.

input: Tell me about Sebastian Müller (Sebastian-20.pdf) — what has his career at ROSEN and Rosenxt looked like?
expected_answer_summary: Sebastian Müller (Sebastian-20.pdf) spent 9 years 4 months at ROSEN — 6 years as Sensor Developer (2014–2020) and then 3+ years as Head of Technology Platform (2020–2024). He transitioned to CTO of Rosenxt Group in October 2023, a role he has held since (2+ years). His academic training — Diplom-Ingenieur in Laser Technology and Optics, M.S. in Photonics, and Ph.D. — gives him strong deep-technology credentials. His LinkedIn top skills are Business Development and Consulting, indicating a transition toward strategic technology leadership.
